patterns.response: **Best beach right now:** Skaket Beach in Orleans. Bay-side, water is 66°F, parking still wide open per latest update. Low tide hits at 2:14pm — perfect timing to walk a half-mile out on the flats. Free parking after 5pm. If you want surf, head to Marconi Beach in Wellfleet instead (41 spots open, 64°F).

**Locals' lobster roll pick:** If Arnold's on Route 6 has an hour-long line, head to **PJ's** instead — the Wellfleet favorite now has a second spot in Eastham (it took over the old Friendly Fisherman). Solid roll, way shorter wait — order it cold with mayo, not hot with butter. Up Cape, **Sesuit Harbor Café in Dennis** is the sleeper hit — BYOB, outdoor harbor seating, $24 roll.

**Rainy afternoon plan:**

• **Sandwich Glass Museum** (Sandwich · $12 · glassblowing demo at 3pm)
• **Cape Cod Aquarium** in Brewster if you've got kids ($8)
• **JFK Hyannis Museum** ($15) — the First Family on the Cape
• Cinnamon buns at **Hot Chocolate Sparrow in Orleans** to round it out

If you're up Cape, the **Provincetown Art Association Museum** is great rainy-day art.

**Family plan, kids in tow:** Head to **Skaket Beach in Orleans** — at low tide the flats stretch a half-mile and kids will lose their minds chasing hermit crabs. After: **Sundae School Ice Cream in Dennis** for the hot fudge ($6). If it's hot, **Cape Cod Inflatable Park in East Wareham** is the all-day winner. **Adventure Isle in South Yarmouth** for mini-golf + go-karts is great evening fun.

**Tonight's sunset is 8:02 PM.** Best spot: **Skaket Beach in Orleans** — bay-side, low tide flats reflect the light. Get there by 7:15pm. Free parking after 5pm.

Alternatives:
• **Race Point Beach in P-Town** — the dramatic one
• **Rock Harbor in Orleans** — bay-side, parking right at the water
• **Mayflower Beach in Dennis** — family-friendly + ice cream nearby

**Locals' breakfast picks:**

• **Spoon and Seed in Hyannis** — smoked salmon hash, opens 7am
• **PB Boulangerie in Wellfleet** — French bakery, worth the line
• **Hole in One Donuts in Eastham** — Cape Sunday tradition
• **Marshside in East Dennis** — water-view Sunday brunch

For coffee on the run: **Hot Chocolate Sparrow in Orleans** — cinnamon buns + iced lattes.

**Tonight's music:**

• 🎵 **The Wailers at Beachcomber (Wellfleet)** — 9pm, $25 cover
• 🎷 **Stone Highway at The Squire (Chatham)** — 8:30pm, $10
• 🎤 **Open Mic at The Mews (P-Town)** — 8pm, free
• 🎺 **Sunset Concert · Chatham Bandstand** — 7:30pm, free (Tom Yates Band)
• 🍷 **Wine + Jazz at Truro Vineyards** — 6-9pm, $15 flight

Check the Today tab for the full updated list.

**Happy hour right now:**

• **Sundancers (Dennis)** — half-off oysters til 6pm ($1.50 each)
• **Aqua Grille (Sandwich)** — $5 cocktails 4-6pm
• **The Squire (Chatham)** — $3 drafts til 6pm

The Sundancers deck is open and oyster prices are unreal — that's where I'd go.

**Whale sightings today:** 3 humpbacks at Stellwagen Bank North reported 48 min ago. A pair was breaching at Race Point Channel this morning. The fastest way to see them is the **Provincetown Whale Watch** boat from MacMillan Pier — $65/adult, 3.5 hour trip, sightings guaranteed or you ride again free. Best afternoon trip leaves at 2pm. Check the Whale Spotter tab for live updates.

**Dinner picks tonight by vibe:**

• **Splurge:** Mac's Shack in Wellfleet — fresh oysters, raw bar, $$$
• **Local:** The Bookstore & Restaurant in Wellfleet — locals' joint
• **Bay views:** Sesuit Harbor Café in Dennis — BYOB outdoor harbor
• **Quick:** PJ's in Eastham — fried seafood & lobster rolls
• **Romantic:** The Lobster Pot in P-Town — sunset water views
• **Family:** Cobie's Clam Shack in Brewster — roadside classic

**Current bridges:**
• Sagamore: 12 min delay (moderate)
• Bourne: 5 min delay (light, wide open right now)

**Tonight's projection:** Sagamore peaks at 48 min around 5pm. Bourne stays under 30. If you're heading off Cape, leave by 5:42pm to beat the worst — or wait until after 7pm.

**This Sunday:** Worst delay 62 min at 11am. Leave before 8am or after 7pm. Bourne is consistently lighter than Sagamore — use it.

**Cape Cod lighthouses worth visiting today:**

• **Nauset Light (Eastham)** — the iconic red-and-white one, free audio tour in the app
• **Highland Light (Truro)** — oldest on the Cape, 1797, museum on site
• **Race Point (P-Town)** — most remote, requires beach walk
• **Nobska Point (Falmouth)** — easiest access, drive right up
• **Chatham Light** — best sunset spot, walking distance from town

Download Pro to unlock all 12 audio tours.

**Free Cape Cod tonight:**

• Chatham Bandstand sunset concert — 7:30pm
• Wellfleet Gallery Walk — 5-8pm, 15+ galleries
• Movie on the Lawn in Hyannis — 8pm at Aselton Park
• Open Mic at The Mews (P-Town) — 8pm
• P-Town Aquarium seal feeding — daily at 2pm

Always free: every National Seashore beach off-season, Cape Cod Rail Trail, Province Lands dunes, Bass Hole Boardwalk in Yarmouth.

**Outer Cape essentials:**

• **Wellfleet:** Mac's Shack, oyster farm tour, Cahoon Hollow Beach + Beachcomber
• **Truro:** Highland Light, Truro Vineyards, Head of Meadow Beach
• **P-Town:** Race Point sunset, Pilgrim Monument, Crown & Anchor cabaret, Lobster Pot dinner

Don't miss the **Wellfleet Drive-In** — open tonight showing Top Gun Maverick, $14/car, cash only.

**Today's tides (Chatham reference):**
• High: 3:48 AM (9.2 ft)
• Low: 10:14 AM (-0.4 ft)
• High: 4:22 PM (9.5 ft)
• Low: 10:38 PM (-0.2 ft)

For tide-flat walking go to Skaket or Mayflower around 2pm. For shellfishing, that's a great low tide. Surf cast at the incoming tide between 11am and 3pm.
